howItWorks.intro: This container load calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Max by volume: product of floor divisions in each dimension
Max by weight: Container max payload ÷ box weight
Practical load ≈ 85% of theoretical max (stacking, access)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is the internal size of a 40ft container?
faq.answer: 40ft standard: 39'5" × 7'8" × 7'10" (474 × 93 × 93 inches). 40ft high cube: same length and width but 102" tall internally. Usable volume: ~2,390 cu ft (standard) or ~2,694 cu ft (high cube).

faq.question: How much weight can a shipping container hold?
faq.answer: 20ft container: max payload ~47,900 lbs. 40ft standard: ~58,600 lbs. However, road weight limits (44,000 lbs for truck + container) often limit you to 39,000-44,000 lbs of cargo.

faq.question: What is the difference between FCL and LCL?
faq.answer: FCL (Full Container Load) means you rent the whole container. LCL (Less than Container Load) shares space with other shippers. FCL is cheaper per unit when you can fill 50%+ of a container.
